response.justification: Bogdan brings a strong generalist background with genuine developer-tooling instincts — he shipped a test runner, assertion library, and IDE components at National Instruments — but the role demands deep Java/Kotlin/JVM expertise and hands-on Spring ecosystem experience that his resume simply does not demonstrate. His primary stack throughout the last decade is TypeScript/Node.js, Java appears only as a footnote in skills and in brief mentions at Altom, and there is no evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or JVM internals work anywhere in his history.
strengths: Directly shipped IDE tooling at National Instruments (NI LabWindows/CVI), showing genuine prior exposure to the developer-tools domain
Strong track record building open-source developer infrastructure (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts, MCP code generators) that signals authentic interest in tooling quality
Demonstrated ability to work across complex, high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP req/s at Dunnhumby) and apply engineering craftsmanship practices like TDD and trunk-based development
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin depth — Java is listed in a skills tag but is never a featured technology in any role description, and Kotlin is absent entirely
Zero Spring ecosystem experience — Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, and enterprise JVM frameworks are unmentioned across 15+ years of employment history
The one IDE role (NI LabWindows/CVI) was C/C++ for a C IDE, not JVM-based tooling, making it a weak proxy for the IntelliJ/JVM plugin development this position requires

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
